markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ confirmation of a November 19, 2026 release date for GTA VI, following two prior delays to prioritize polish, remains the central driver of market sentiment. Take-Two has signaled that major marketing will ramp up this summer, positioning an August window for the next trailer as the most immediate catalyst traders are watching. With roughly five months until launch, attention centers on streaming metrics, pre-order trends, and any additional gameplay reveals that could shape pre-release hype. Historical patterns show Rockstar’s deliberate rollout strategy often rewards patient marketing, while any last-minute scope adjustments could still introduce volatility in the final stretch.

No recent updates suggest acceleration, and the console-focused launch strategy remains unchanged. While last-minute shifts occur in major entertainment releases, the binding corporate commitments and absence of new trailers or pre-order activity reinforce the overwhelming market-implied odds against an earlier debut.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial has sharply boosted the “No Prison Time” outcome to 74.5% implied probability. A Manhattan jury deadlocked on the Jessica Mann rape charge in mid-May 2026, leaving prosecutors weighing a potential fourth trial while Weinstein already serves a 16-year California sentence for prior convictions. Any additional New York term from the pending criminal-sexual-act conviction or new proceedings would likely run concurrently or be limited by time served, health factors, and appeals, reducing the chance of meaningful extra incarceration. The lower-probability brackets for 5–30+ years reflect residual risk of a successful retrial or consecutive sentencing, but the hung jury and procedural fatigue have tilted trader consensus toward minimal or no further time.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes enter the 2026 Stanley Cup Final as the clear market leaders after a dominant 53-22-7 regular season that secured the top Eastern Conference seed and Metropolitan Division title. Their 12-1 postseason record, featuring strong defensive play and contributions from forwards like Sebastian Aho, Seth Jarvis, and Andrei Svechnikov, has reinforced trader consensus on their implied probability. The Vegas Golden Knights reached the Final by sweeping the Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Finals under new head coach John Tortorella, leveraging a balanced roster and goaltending to overcome a middling regular-season finish. This head-to-head setup, with both teams showing recent momentum and roster health, explains their combined dominance in the futures market over other eliminated contenders.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder hold the highest implied probability for the 2026 NBA title due to their league-best 64-18 regular-season record and top Western Conference seed, bolstered by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Chet Holmgren's All-NBA and All-Defense honors amid strong depth and defensive metrics. The New York Knicks follow closely on the strength of their 53-29 mark, third Eastern seed, and historic playoff dominance—including an 11-game winning streak with multiple double-digit victories across early rounds against the Hawks, 76ers, and Cavaliers. The San Antonio Spurs sit third, driven by Victor Wembanyama's franchise-record performances, unanimous All-Defense selection, and All-NBA First Team nod, as they push the Thunder to a decisive Game 7 in the Western Conference Finals after splitting the prior six games. These outcomes reflect the wisdom of crowds pricing recent form, roster health, and conference paths.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain, France, and England sit atop the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market as the leading European contenders, with implied probabilities clustered tightly between 11% and 17% amid strong recent form and squad depth. Spain’s status as the most recent European champions, combined with an unbeaten run in competitive matches stretching back over two years, underpins its narrow edge, though Lamine Yamal’s April hamstring injury introduced minor uncertainty around attacking options. France benefits from its 2022 final appearance, consistent qualification results, and proven knockout experience under Didier Deschamps, while England’s back-to-back European Championship final runs highlight sustained progress despite a recent dip in friendlies. The expanded 48-team field and one-month lead-in to the June 11 opener in North America leave room for momentum shifts from injuries or group-stage results, keeping the race among these sides and Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal highly competitive.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain, France, and England sit atop the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market as the leading European contenders, with implied probabilities clustered tightly between 11% and 17% amid strong recent form and squad depth. Spain’s status as the most recent European champions, combined with an unbeaten run in competitive matches stretching back over two years, underpins its narrow edge, though Lamine Yamal’s April hamstring injury introduced minor uncertainty around attacking options. France benefits from its 2022 final appearance, consistent qualification results, and proven knockout experience under Didier Deschamps, while England’s back-to-back European Championship final runs highlight sustained progress despite a recent dip in friendlies. The expanded 48-team field and one-month lead-in to the June 11 opener in North America leave room for momentum shifts from injuries or group-stage results, keeping the race among these sides and Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal highly competitive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain, France, and England sit atop the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market as the leading European contenders, with implied probabilities clustered tightly between 11% and 17% amid strong recent form and squad depth. The expanded 48-team field and one-month lead-in to the June 11 opener in North America leave room for momentum shifts from injuries or group-stage results, keeping the race among these sides and Brazil, Argentina, and Portugal highly competitive.
